data.description:
Add failing (RED-phase) tests to tests/scripts/test-ci-llm-review-runner.sh covering the deep-tier dispatch path in plugins/dso/scripts/ci-llm-review-runner.sh.

Tests to add (all must FAIL before Task 2 is implemented):

1. test_runner_deep_tier_dispatches_three_specialists — when classifier returns selected_tier=deep, runner makes exactly 3 separate API calls (one per specialist: correctness, verification, hygiene), each with the corresponding agent file as system prompt; assert three slot files are written to WORKFLOW_PLUGIN_ARTIFACTS_DIR: reviewer-findings-correctness.json, reviewer-findings-verification.json, reviewer-findings-hygiene.json.

2. test_runner_deep_tier_arch_synthesis_is_final_writer — after the 3 specialist calls, runner makes a 4th API call using code-reviewer-deep-arch.md as system prompt; arch response is written as reviewer-findings.json (sole final writer); specialist slot files remain present alongside it.

3. test_runner_deep_tier_fail_closed_on_missing_slot — when one specialist slot file is absent (simulate by having that specialist curl mock exit non-zero), runner exits non-zero without calling the arch agent.

4. test_runner_deep_tier_fail_closed_on_malformed_slot — when a specialist slot file contains invalid JSON, runner exits non-zero before the arch synthesis step.

Test infrastructure: use PATH-injection mock pattern already established in the file (MOCK dirs, _create_mock_curl helper). For multi-call discrimination, write a curl mock that inspects the --data-raw body to determine which specialist is being called (key off the agent filename embedded in the system prompt) and writes the appropriate slot file. Done criteria: all 4 tests exist, are named as above, and FAIL with a clear assertion error (not an unexpected crash) when run against the current runner.
data.title: RED: add deep-tier dispatch tests to test-ci-llm-review-runner.sh
